openmeter/meterevent/adapter.purpose: Implements meterevent.Service by composing streaming.Connector (ClickHouse), customer.Service, and meter.Service — no Ent/Postgres access. Resolves customer filters early, delegates raw queries to the streaming layer, then runs a fixed post-process pipeline (map → enrich → validate).

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: Declare var _ meterevent.Service = (*adapter)(nil) in adapter.go to enforce compile-time conformance.

patterns.name: Validate-before-delegate
patterns.description: ListEvents and ListEventsV2 call params.Validate() and wrap any error in models.NewGenericValidationError before reaching the streaming connector.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("validate input: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: Fixed post-process pipeline
patterns.description: After fetching raw events always call eventPostProcess which runs mapEventsToMeterEvents → enrichEventsWithCustomerID → validateEvents in that order; never skip or reorder steps.

patterns.name: Early-empty-return on customer filter miss
patterns.description: If a customerIDs filter is supplied but listCustomers returns zero results, return an empty slice immediately without querying the streaming layer.

patterns.name: Subject→customerID per-call cache
patterns.description: enrichEventsWithCustomerID builds a map[string]string cache keyed by subject to avoid repeated DB lookups within a single call; new enrichment helpers must replicate this.
patterns.example: cache := make(map[string]string); if customerID, ok := cache[event.Subject]; ok { ... }

patterns.name: Cursor emitted only on full page
patterns.description: In ListEventsV2, set result.NextCursor only when len(meterEvents) == effectiveLimit; leave it nil for partial pages.
patterns.example: if len(meterEvents) > 0 && len(meterEvents) == effectiveLimit { cursor := meterEvents[len(meterEvents)-1].Cursor(); result.NextCursor = &cursor }

patterns.name: ValidationErrors attached per-event, not returned
patterns.description: validateEvents appends errors to event.ValidationErrors for each invalid event and still appends the event to the output; it never returns a top-level error for individual event failures.

key_file_guides.role: Constructor New() and struct holding streamingConnector, customerService, meterService only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add an Ent/DB client here — this adapter is streaming-only; persistence imports break domain isolation.

key_file_guides.role: All method implementations: ListEvents, ListEventsV2, listCustomers, eventPostProcess, mapEventsToMeterEvents, validateEvents, enrichEventsWithCustomerID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: enrichEventsWithCustomerID has a FIXME: one DB call per event; the cache mitigates N+1 within one call but batching is a TODO — do not add more per-event service calls without a cache.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db or any Ent-generated package — this adapter never touches Postgres.
Returning a top-level error from validateEvents for individual event failures — use Event.ValidationErrors.
Skipping params.Validate() before delegating to the streaming connector.
Emitting NextCursor when the result page is smaller than effectiveLimit.
Adding additional per-event service calls inside eventPostProcess without a per-call cache.

decisions.decision: Implemented as adapter/ rather than a service/ sub-package
decisions.rationale: meterevent has no independent business logic; it purely composes streaming queries with customer/meter lookups, so a distinct service layer would be empty indirection.

decisions.decision: ValidationErrors are per-event fields, not top-level errors
decisions.rationale: Event listing is a query API; callers need all events including partially-invalid ones, so failing the whole request for one bad event would break observability workflows.

code_examples.scenario: Resolving a domain-service filter before querying the streaming layer

openmeter/meterevent/httphandler.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the meterevent domain, adapting v1 (ListEvents) and v2 (ListEventsV2) REST endpoints to meterevent.Service calls via the generic httptransport.HandlerWithArgs pipeline. Mounted by openmeter/server/router.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs triple: decoder / operation / encoder
patterns.description: Every endpoint is built with httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Response](http.StatusOK), options...); the decoder maps params only, the operation calls the service.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(func(ctx, r, params) (Req, error) {...}, func(ctx, req) (Resp, error) {...}, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[ListEventsResponse](http.StatusOK), opts...)

patterns.name: Type-alias block per endpoint file
patterns.description: Each handler file opens with a type block aliasing Params/Response to api.* and Request to meterevent.* params, plus the Handler alias.
patterns.example: type ( ListEventsParams = api.ListEventsParams; ListEventsResponse = []api.IngestedEvent; ListEventsHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...] ); type ListEventsRequest = meterevent.ListEventsParams

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via resolveNamespace in decoder
patterns.description: The decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first; namespace is never a query param and a missing namespace returns http.StatusInternalServerError (server misconfiguration, not 400).
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListEventsRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: Validation errors wrapped in decoder, not operation
patterns.description: Param-conversion errors (e.g. from convertListEventsV2Params) are wrapped in models.NewGenericValidationError in the decoder before returning.
patterns.example: if err != nil { return ListEventsV2Request{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: WithOperationName appended to handler options
patterns.description: Every handler appends httptransport.WithOperationName("<camelCaseName>") via httptransport.AppendOptions for tracing/metrics labeling.

patterns.name: Mapping functions in mapping.go, not inline
patterns.description: All API↔domain conversions (convertEvent, convertListEventsV2Params, convertListEventsV2Response) live in mapping.go; handler files call them with no inline conversion.

patterns.name: Handler interface embeds fine-grained sub-interfaces
patterns.description: The exported Handler interface embeds EventHandler (and future groups); new endpoint groups become new embedded interfaces, not direct methods on Handler.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler/EventHandler interfaces, the private handler struct, New() constructor, and resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveNamespace returns http.StatusInternalServerError for a missing namespace — this is correct (server misconfiguration); do not change to 400.

key_file_guides.role: ListEvents handler (v1); applies MaximumFromDuration and MaximumLimit defaults in the decoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: minimumFrom uses MaximumFromDuration to avoid edge-case validation failures — preserve when changing time window logic.

key_file_guides.role: ListEventsV2 handler (v2); delegates param conversion to convertListEventsV2Params in mapping.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: StoredAt filter is not forwarded through convertListEventsV2Params — adding it requires changes in both mapping.go and the domain params struct.

key_file_guides.role: All API↔domain conversions: convertEvent, convertListEventsV2Params, convertListEventsV2Response.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidationErrors are joined with errors.Join into api.IngestedEvent.ValidationError as *string (nil when none) — never returned as top-level errors.

anti_patterns: Calling meterevent.Service methods in the decoder — decoder maps params only; service calls belong in the operation.
Inlining type conversion logic in handler files instead of mapping.go.
Omitting WithOperationName from handler options — breaks tracing and metrics labeling.
Adding business logic (e.g. time window enforcement) in the operation instead of the decoder.
Returning a non-200 status from JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus for successful responses — errors go through the error encoder chain.

decisions.decision: v1 and v2 list handlers live in separate files (event.go vs event_v2.go)
decisions.rationale: v1 returns a flat slice with a hard default limit; v2 uses cursor pagination and richer filters — separate files avoid branching inside one handler and keep versioned behavior auditable.
Keeps handler files focused on the decode→operate→encode lifecycle and makes conversions independently testable without reading handler code.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v2-style list endpoint to this handler package
